paper.title: Unsupervised Evaluation of Interactive Dialog with DialoGPT

checks.question: Does the evaluation script match the paper's reported numbers exactly, or are there post-processing differences?
checks.notes: Repo contains only fed.py/fed_demo.py/README.md with no explicit evaluation script or paper-number comparison harness; question is out of scope without additional files (e.g., reproduction scripts or reference metrics) to compare against.

checks.question: Are there any hard-coded model checkpoints or tokenizer versions that could cause the results to drift over time?
checks.notes: The default model/tokenizer is hard-coded to the HuggingFace `microsoft/DialoGPT-large` checkpoint (with a `<|endoftext|>` token assumption), so upstream changes to that repo or the transformers API could cause scoring drift over time.

checks.question: Does this repo contain an eval.py or analogous entrypoint? If so, what does it compute?
checks.notes: "No eval.py, but `fed.py` defines `evaluate()` (called from `fed_demo.py`) which computes turn- and dialog-level FED metric scores as the difference between a language model's loss on low-quality versus high-quality follow-up utterances for each metric.
